title: Fermented Crispy German Cherry Flatbread
description: A lowcarb, flaky flatbread made with a fermented almondcoconut dough, topped with marinated cherries and fresh rosemary for a decadent Germanstyle snack.
instructions: Combine almond flour, coconut flour, xanthan gum, and salt in a bowl. Stir well.
In a separate bowl, mix warm water, sourdough starter, and olive oil until smooth. Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and knead until a soft, elastic dough forms.
Cover the dough and leave it to ferment at room temperature for 12 hours, or until it doubles in size and shows bubbles.
Preheat the oven to 220 °C (428 °F). On a parchment‑lined sheet, roll out the dough into a 30 cm square, about 5 mm thick.
While the dough rests, whisk together cherries, cherry jam, apple cider vinegar, and a pinch of pepper. Spread the cherry mixture evenly over the dough.
Sprinkle chopped rosemary over the cherries. Drizzle a little olive oil for extra crispness.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, or until edges are golden and the surface is blistered. Let cool for a few minutes before cutting into squares.
Serve warm or at room temperature as a flavorful, low‑carb snack.
